verses.latin_vulgate: Addidit quoque Job, assumens parabolam suam, et dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣סֶף אִ֭יּוֹב שְׂאֵ֥ת מְשָׁל֗וֹ וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Job also added, taking up his parable, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Job puhui taas sananlaskunsa ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi tribuat ut sim juxta menses pristinos,  secundum dies quibus Deus custodiebat me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־יִתְּנֵ֥נִי כְיַרְחֵי־קֶ֑דֶם כִּ֝ימֵ֗י אֱל֣וֹהַּ יִשְׁמְרֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who will grant me, that I might be according to the months past, according to the days in which God kept me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ah jos minä olisin niinkuin entisinä kuukausina! niinä päivinä, joina Jumala minun kätki,
verses.king_james: Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;

verses.latin_vulgate: Quando splendebat lucerna ejus super caput meum,  et ad lumen ejus ambulabam in tenebris :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּהִלּ֣וֹ נֵ֭רוֹ עֲלֵ֣י רֹאשִׁ֑י לְ֝אוֹר֗וֹ אֵ֣לֶךְ חֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: When his lamp shined over my head, and I walked by his light in darkness?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska hänen valkeutensa paisti minun pääni päälle, ja minä kävin pimeissä hänen valkeudessansa,
verses.king_james: When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut fui in diebus adolescentiæ meæ,  quando secreto Deus erat in tabernaculo meo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר הָ֭יִיתִי בִּימֵ֣י חׇרְפִּ֑י בְּס֥וֹד אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ עֲלֵ֣י אׇהֳלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: As I was in the days of my youth, when God was secretly in my tabernacle?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin minä olin nuorena olessani; koska Jumalan salaisuus oli minun majani päällä;
verses.king_james: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;

verses.latin_vulgate: quando erat Omnipotens mecum,  et in circuitu meo pueri mei :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּע֣וֹד שַׁ֭דַּי עִמָּדִ֑י סְבִ֖יבוֹתַ֣י נְעָרָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: When the Almighty was with me: and my servants round about me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Kaikkivaltias oli vielä minun kanssani, ja minun nuorukaiseni minun ympärilläni;
verses.king_james: When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;

verses.latin_vulgate: quando lavabam pedes meos butyro,  et petra fundebat mihi rivos olei :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְחֹ֣ץ הֲלִיכַ֣י בְּחֵמָ֑ה וְצ֥וּר יָצ֥וּק עִ֝מָּדִ֗י פַּלְגֵי־שָֽׁמֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: When I washed my feet with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska minä pesin minun tieni voilla, ja kallio vuoti minulle öljy-ojat;
verses.king_james: When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;

verses.latin_vulgate: quando procedebam ad portam civitatis,  et in platea parabant cathedram mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּצֵ֣אתִי שַׁ֣עַר עֲלֵי־קָ֑רֶת בָּ֝רְח֗וֹב אָכִ֥ין מוֹשָׁבִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: When I went out to the gate of the city, and in the street they prepared me a chair?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska minä menin kaupunin porteille, ja annoin valmistaa istuimeni kujille;
verses.king_james: When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!

verses.latin_vulgate: Videbant me juvenes, et abscondebantur :  et senes assurgentes stabant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָא֣וּנִי נְעָרִ֣ים וְנֶחְבָּ֑אוּ וִ֝ישִׁישִׁ֗ים קָ֣מוּ עָמָֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the old men rose up and stood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin nuoret näkivät minun, niin he pakenivat, ja vanhat nousivat ja seisoivat minun edessäni,
verses.king_james: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes cessabant loqui,  et digitum superponebant ori suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׂ֭רִים עָצְר֣וּ בְמִלִּ֑ים וְ֝כַ֗ף יָשִׂ֥ימוּ לְפִיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The princes ceased to speak, and laid the finger on their mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ylimmäiset lakkasivat puhumasta, ja panivat kätensä suunsa päälle,
verses.king_james: The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocem suam cohibebant duces,  et lingua eorum gutturi suo adhærebat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוֹל־נְגִידִ֥ים נֶחְבָּ֑אוּ וּ֝לְשׁוֹנָ֗ם לְחִכָּ֥ם דָּבֵֽקָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The rulers held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to their throat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ruhtinasten ääni kätkeysi, ja heidän kielensä suun lakeen tarttui.
verses.king_james: The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auris audiens beatificabat me,  et oculus videns testimonium reddebat mihi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֹ֣זֶן שָׁ֭מְעָה וַֽתְּאַשְּׁרֵ֑נִי וְעַ֥יִן רָ֝אֲתָ֗ה וַתְּעִידֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The ear that heard me blessed me, and the eye that saw me gave witness to me:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä kenen korva minun kuuli, se kiitti minua onnelliseksi, ja jonka silmä minun näki, se todisti minusta.
verses.king_james: When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:

verses.latin_vulgate: eo quod liberassem pauperem vociferantem,  et pupillum cui non esset adjutor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אֲ֭מַלֵּט עָנִ֣י מְשַׁוֵּ֑עַ וְ֝יָת֗וֹם וְֽלֹא־עֹזֵ֥ר לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because I had delivered the poor man that cried out; and the fatherless, that had no helper.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä autin köyhää, joka huusi, ja orpoa, jolla ei auttajaa ollut.
verses.king_james: Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Benedictio perituri super me veniebat,  et cor viduæ consolatus sum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְכַּ֣ת אֹ֭בֵד עָלַ֣י תָּבֹ֑א וְלֵ֖ב אַלְמָנָ֣ה אַרְנִֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me, and I comforted the heart of the widow.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niiden siunaus, jotka katoomallansa olivat, tuli minun päälleni; ja minä ilahutin leskein sydämen.
verses.king_james: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justitia indutus sum,  et vestivi me, sicut vestimento et diademate, judicio meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צֶ֣דֶק לָ֭בַשְׁתִּי וַיִּלְבָּשֵׁ֑נִי כִּֽמְעִ֥יל וְ֝צָנִ֗יף מִשְׁפָּטִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I was clad with justice: and I clothed myself with my judgment, as with a robe and a diadem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vanhurskaus oli minun pukuni, jonka minä päälleni puin, ja minun oikeuteni oli minulle niinkuin hame ja kaunistus.
verses.king_james: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculus fui cæco, et pes claudo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינַ֣יִם הָ֭יִיתִי לַעִוֵּ֑ר וְרַגְלַ֖יִם לַפִּסֵּ֣חַ אָֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I was an eye to the blind, and a foot to the lame.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olin sokian silmä ja ontuvan jalka.
verses.king_james: I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pater eram pauperum,  et causam quam nesciebam diligentissime investigabam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ב אָ֭נֹכִֽי לָאֶבְיוֹנִ֑ים וְרִ֖ב לֹא־יָדַ֣עְתִּי אֶחְקְרֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I was the father of the poor: and the cause which I knew not, I searched out most diligently.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olin köyhäin isä, ja jonka asiaa en minä ymmärtänyt, sen minä visusti tutkin.
verses.king_james: I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conterebam molas iniqui,  et de dentibus illius auferebam prædam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֭אֲשַׁבְּרָה מְתַלְּע֣וֹת עַוָּ֑ל וּ֝מִשִּׁנָּ֗יו אַשְׁלִ֥יךְ טָֽרֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: I broke the jaws of the wicked man, and out of his teeth I took away the prey.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä särjin väärän syömähampaat, ja otin saaliin hänen hampaistansa,
verses.king_james: And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebamque : In nidulo meo moriar,  et sicut palma multiplicabo dies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֭אֹמַר עִם־קִנִּ֣י אֶגְוָ֑ע וְ֝כַח֗וֹל אַרְבֶּ֥ה יָמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I said: I shall die in my nest, and as a palm tree shall multiply my days.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä ajattelin: minä riuduin pesässäni, ja teen päiväni moneksi niinkuin sannan.
verses.king_james: Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Radix mea aperta est secus aquas,  et ros morabitur in messione mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׇׁרְשִׁ֣י פָת֣וּחַ אֱלֵי־מָ֑יִם וְ֝טַ֗ל יָלִ֥ין בִּקְצִירִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My root is opened beside the waters, and dew shall continue in my harvest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun juureni putkahti veden tykönä, ja kaste pysyi laihoni päällä.
verses.king_james: My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gloria mea semper innovabitur,  et arcus meus in manu mea instaurabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭בוֹדִי חָדָ֣שׁ עִמָּדִ֑י וְ֝קַשְׁתִּ֗י בְּיָדִ֥י תַחֲלִֽיף׃
verses.douay_rheims: My glory shall always be renewed, and my bow in my hand shall be repaired.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun kunniallisuuteni uudistui minun edessäni, ja minun joutseni muuttui uudeksi minun kädessäni.
verses.king_james: My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui me audiebant, expectabant sententiam,  et intenti tacebant ad consilium meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִֽי־שָׁמְע֥וּ וְיִחֵ֑לּוּ וְ֝יִדְּמ֗וּ לְמ֣וֹ עֲצָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that heard me, waited for my sentence, and being attentive held their peace at my counsel.
verses.finnish_biblia: He kuulivat minua ja odottivat, ja vaikenivat minun neuvooni.
verses.king_james: Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbis meis addere nihil audebant,  et super illos stillabat eloquium meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַחֲרֵ֣י דְ֭בָרִי לֹ֣א יִשְׁנ֑וּ וְ֝עָלֵ֗ימוֹ תִּטֹּ֥ף מִלָּתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: To my words they durst add nothing, and my speech dropped upon them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun sanani jälkeen ei yksikään enempää puhunut, ja minun puheeni tiukkui heidän päällensä.
verses.king_james: After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Expectabant me sicut pluviam,  et os suum aperiebant quasi ad imbrem serotinum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִחֲל֣וּ כַמָּטָ֣ר לִ֑י וּ֝פִיהֶ֗ם פָּעֲר֥וּ לְמַלְקֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They waited for me as for rain, and they opened their mouth as for a latter shower.
verses.finnish_biblia: He odottivat minua niinkuin sadetta, ja avasivat suunsa niinkuin ehtoosadetta vastaan.
verses.king_james: And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Siquando ridebam ad eos, non credebant :  et lux vultus mei non cadebat in terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶשְׂחַ֣ק אֲ֭לֵהֶם לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ינוּ וְא֥וֹר פָּ֝נַ֗י לֹ֣א יַפִּילֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: If at any time I laughed on them, they believed not, and the light of my countenance fell not on earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä nauroin heidän puoleensa, ei he luottaneet sen päälle, eikä tahtoneet minua murheesen saattaa.
verses.king_james: If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si voluissem ire ad eos, sedebam primus :  cumque sederem quasi rex, circumstante exercitu,  eram tamen mœrentium consolator.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֥בְחַ֣ר דַּרְכָּם֮ וְאֵשֵׁ֢ב רֹ֥֫אשׁ וְ֭אֶשְׁכּוֹן כְּמֶ֣לֶךְ בַּגְּד֑וּד כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר אֲבֵלִ֣ים יְנַחֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I had a mind to go to them, I sat first, and when I sat as a king, with his army standing about him, yet I was a comforter of them that mourned.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin minä tulin heidän kokouksiinsa, niin minun täytyi istua ylimpänä: ja asuin niinkuin kuningas sotaväen keskellä, lohduttaissani murheellisia.
verses.king_james: I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.
